task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI is running and the correct playback file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible, and the playback file name 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt' is displayed in the control panel or playback widget.

task_completion.description: Verify that the odd-numbered channels (1, 3, 5, 7) are active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Time Series widget or channel controller, channels 1, 3, 5, and 7 are toggled ON and their corresponding EEG data traces are visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the even-numbered channels (2, 4, 6, 8) are deactivated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Time Series widget or channel controller, channels 2, 4, 6, and 8 are toggled OFF, and their corresponding data traces are hidden or removed from the display.

task_completion.description: Verify that the time series display window is set to 10 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget's time window setting (often located at the bottom or in the widget settings) displays '10 sec' or the x-axis clearly spans 10 seconds.

task_completion.description: Verify that the screenshot of the montage was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal, file manager, or GUI notification shows that a file named 'left_hemi_montage.png' has been saved in the '~/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/' directory.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic OpenBCI GUI interface with its standard widgets (Time Series, FFT, etc.), not a text editor modifying configuration files or a mocked UI.

integrity.description: Ensure the playback file is genuinely running and displaying data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The active channels in the Time Series widget show actual fluctuating EEG waveforms, not perfectly flat lines or static placeholder images.

integrity.description: Ensure the screenshot was not faked using terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent using commands like 'convert' or 'touch' to create a dummy image file named 'left_hemi_montage.png' without actually taking a screenshot of the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct recording file is 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-MotorImagery.txt'. The target channels to be ON are 1, 3, 5, and 7. The target channels to be OFF are 2, 4, 6, and 8. The time window duration must be exactly 10 seconds. The final screenshot must be saved at '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/left_hemi_montage.png'.
